phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Slight deficit at level 1: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your root-rotation sieging on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the point-blank root answers anyone who closes. Trade Q-W-Q only when his engage tool is down.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E unlocks the real combo: E-Q flux chunks and the wave-bounce clears. The lane starts being yours to play.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Tear + Lost Chapter and the EQ machine hums — short-trade his every wave contact and walk away before the answer.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your root-rotation sieging on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: RoA spike: your combo chunks half bars and your root starts kill chains. Force the trades you spent five levels avoiding.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Slight edge at two-plus items: your root-rotation sieging beat Anivia's wall-frost poke control on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.why: Two items and you ARE the win condition: EQ deletes carries, W stops divers, and Realm Warp turns objectives into ambushes.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-farm at max range — your level 1 is honest at best. Bank Tear stacks and give nothing away.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W online: the point-blank root answers anyone who closes. Trade Q-W-Q only when his engage tool is down.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E unlocks the real combo: E-Q flux chunks and the wave-bounce clears. The lane starts being yours to play.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight deficit through levels 4-5: Yone's double-blade spirit dives edge out your root-rotation sieging on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Realm Warp online — you are now a map play waiting to happen. The lane is a timer: shove and warp somewhere profitable.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Yone's double-blade spirit dives own this window — your root-rotation sieging don't answer them on first item yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean your way — root-rotation sieging edge out the double-blade spirit dives when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.
